02 May 2026 Military News

Operations
Defense Policy / Programs
Defense Industry
Other Conflicts
News Reports

Current Operations

    Other Operations

    Operation Epic Fury / Operation Roaring Lion | Operation True Promise 4

  • Shipping through Strait of Hormuz falls over 90 pct, 41 security incidents recorded in nearby waters: UK navy People's Daily 02 May 2026 -- The British Royal Navy said on Friday that shipping traffic through the Strait of Hormuz has dropped sharply since the outbreak of conflict in the Middle East, with the number of vessels passing through the waterway falling from about 130 per day before the conflict to fewer than 10, a decline of more than 90 percent.

  • May 2: Iran reaffirms Strait of Hormuz control as US pressure backfires and desperation grows Press TV 02 May 2026 -- Iran has reinforced its authority over the Persian Gulf and the Strait of Hormuz as the Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC) Navy moved to operationalise the Leader of the Islamic Revolution, Ayatollah Seyyed Mojtaba Khamenei's new maritime directive.

  • Majority of US bases in West Asia damaged in retaliatory strikes by Iran, allies: Probe Press TV 02 May 2026 -- A new investigation by CNN has found that a large portion of the United States' military installations across West Asia have sustained damage following strikes by Iran and its allies, raising concerns in Washington about the resilience of its regional infrastructure.

  • Trump: 'We're Like Pirates' In Naval Blockade Of Iran RFE/RL 02 May 2026 -- US President Donald Trump on May 1 said the US Navy was acting "like pirates" in its mission to blockade Iranian ports during the US-Israel war with Iran.
  • Trump admits US Navy acting like 'pirates' against Iranian ships Press TV 02 May 2026 -- US President Donald Trump has unabashedly admitted that the US Navy is acting "like pirates" in carrying out Washington's naval blockade of Iranian ports.
  • Trump Tells Congress Iran Hostilities Terminated, US Force Posture Still Being Updated Sputnik 02 May 2026 -- US President Donald Trump said on Friday that hostilities against Iran, which began on February 28, have terminated, though the Pentagon continues to update its military posture in the region as the threat remains.
  • Trump Vows No 'Early' Iran Exit After Saying 'Not Satisfied' With 'Messed Up' Tehran's Proposal RFE/RL 02 May 2026 -- US ⁠President Donald Trump on May 1 said the US would not quit its confrontation with Iran "early" only to face renewed difficulties in the future, hours after he said he was "not satisfied" with the latest peace proposal offered by Tehran through Pakistani mediators.
  • Trump tells lawmakers war against Iran has "terminated" as 60-day deadline arrives People's Daily 02 May 2026 -- U.S. President Donald Trump told lawmakers on Friday that the war against Iran has "terminated," as the military action -- which was launched without Congressional approval -- has reached a 60-day legal deadline.
  • Trump says "not satisfied" with Iran's latest proposal People's Daily 02 May 2026 -- U.S. President Donald Trump said Friday he is "not satisfied" with Iran's latest proposal to end the U.S.-Israeli war with the Middle Eastern country, which is currently under a continuing ceasefire.

  • Senior Iranian Military Figure Says Resumption Of War With US 'Likely' RFE/RL 02 May 2026 -- A senior Islamic Revolutionary Guards Corps commander has said a renewed military confrontation between Iran and the United States is "likely" -- just two days after Iran delivered a draft proposal for a peace deal to mediator Pakistan.
  • IRGC: No power capable of weakening Iranian nation's resolve Press TV 02 May 2026 -- The Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC) expresses gratitude towards the Iranian nation for its resolute defense of the country and its Islamic establishment through tremendous popular solidarity rallies.
  • FM: Repeating clichés about Iran's nuclear activities 'irresponsible' Press TV 02 May 2026 -- Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi has denounced some European states for repeating incorrect clichés concerning Iran's peaceful nuclear energy activities.
  • Iran says 'ball is in US court' after submitting proposal to mediators Press TV 02 May 2026 -- Iran says it has submitted a comprehensive proposal to bring about a permanent end to the US-Israeli aggression against the country, insisting that it is now up to the United States to decide whether it wants a deal or confrontation.
  • No limit on uranium enrichment level under IAEA supervision: Iran's UN mission Press TV 02 May 2026 -- Iran's permanent mission to the United Nations says there is no restriction on the level of uranium enrichment under the IAEA's supervision.

  • UN chief warns of severe consequences of prolonged Hormuz closure People's Daily 02 May 2026 -- UN Secretary-General Antonio Guterres on Thursday warned of devastating consequences of prolonged disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz.

  • Russia's Balancing Act Wobbling as Iran Crisis Deepens, Says Policy Analyst Katz RFE/RL 02 May 2026 -- As tensions across Eastern Europe and the Persian Gulf roll down parallel tracks, a new conventional wisdom is taking hold -- namely, that the wars in Ukraine and the Middle East are no longer isolated events but two fronts in a single, evolving global struggle.

  • Japan-Iran Foreign Ministers' Telephone Call Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Japan 02 May 2026

Defense Policy / Programs

  • Pentagon announces deals with AI companies for classified networks People's Daily 02 May 2026 -- The U.S. Department of Defense said on Friday that it has entered into agreements with eight artificial intelligence companies to deploy advanced AI capabilities on the department's classified networks for operational use.

  • Chairmen of Armed Services Committees Release Statement on Withdrawal of Troops from Germany House Armed Services Committee 02 May 2026 -- U.S. Senator Roger Wicker, (R-MS), Chairman of the Senate Armed Services Committee, and U.S. Representative Mike Rogers, (R-AL), Chairman of the House Armed Services Committee, issued the following statement in response to the Pentagon's decision to withdraw approximately 5,000 troops from Germany and cancel the planned deployment there of a Long-Range Fires Battalion.
  • US Announces Withdrawal Of 5,000 Troops From Germany RFE/RL 02 May 2026 -- The Pentagon has announced plans to withdraw approximately 5,000 US troops from Germany over the next 6-12 months, amid tensions between President Donald Trump and German Chancellor Friedrich Merz over the ongoing war with Iran.
  • Pentagon Confirms Decision to Withdraw 5,000 American Troops From Germany Within Year Sputnik 02 May 2026 -- Chief Pentagon spokesman Sean Parnell confirmed to RIA Novosti the Pentagon's decision to withdraw 5,000 American troops from Germany within a year.

  • U.S. Navy to Christen Future USNS Solomon Atkinson DoD 02 May 2026 -- The U.S. Navy will christen the future USNS Solomon Atkinson (T-ATS 12), during a ceremony at Austal USA on Saturday, May 2, at 10 a.m. CDT.

  • Pentagon warns Europe of arms delays as war on Iran drains stockpiles: Report Press TV 02 May 2026 -- The Pentagon has warned Washington's European allies to expect long delivery delays for US weapons, as it struggles to replenish stockpiles depleted by the illegal war against Iran.

  • NATO 'Working With US' On German Troop Cut RFE/RL 02 May 2026 -- NATO has responded to the US announcement that it will withdraw 5,000 troops from Germany.

  • China's Y-20B strategic cargo aircraft equipped with WS-20 high bypass ratio turbofan engines, achieves superior long-range strategic airlift capability: report Global Times 02 May 2026 -- The newly upgraded Y-20B transport aircraft of the Y-20 fleet, which has recently carried out a mission to repatriate the remains of the 13th batch of Chinese People's Volunteers martyrs from South Korea, features its major core upgrade in the full installation of the larger-diameter domestically developed WS-20 turbofan engines, China Central Television (CCTV) News reported on Saturday.

  • CHIEF OF THE NAVAL STAFF VISIT TO MYANMAR India PIB 02 May 2026 -- Admiral Dinesh K Tripathi, Chief of the Naval Staff, is on a four-day official visit to Myanmar from 02 - 05 May 2026.

  • IDF to Receive Two New Fighter Squadrons: Ministerial Procurement Committee Approves Acquisition of F-35 and F-15IA Squadrons Israel MOD 02 May 2026 -- The Ministerial Committee on Procurement has approved the Israel Ministry of Defense (IMOD)'s plan to simultaneously acquire two new fighter squadrons: a fourth F-35 squadron from Lockheed Martin, and a second F-15IA squadron from Boeing. The deals, valued at tens of billions of NIS, include full fleet integration into the Israeli Air Force, comprehensive sustainment, spare parts, and logistics support.

  • US State Department OKs $8.6 Billion In Military Sales To Israel, Gulf Allies RFE/RL 02 May 2026 -- The US ⁠State ‌Department on May 1 said it has approved sales of military equipment worth more than $8.6 billion to Middle ⁠Eastern allies, including Israel, that have been targeted by Iran in retaliation for the US-Israeli air strikes launched on February 28.

Defense Industry

Other Conflicts


    Ukraine Conflict News

    Operation Iron Swords / Operation Al-Aqsa Flood | Operation Northern Arrows | Giddeon's Chariots | Roaring Lion (Lebanon)

  • Israeli airstrikes kill 12 more civilians in southern Lebanon amid continued ceasefire violations Press TV 02 May 2026 -- Israeli forces have carried out a series of airstrikes targeting multiple areas in Lebanon's Nabatieh governorate, killing a dozen civilians and marking continued violations of a ceasefire agreement that has been in place since April 17.
  • Israeli Military Hits Dozens Of Hezbollah Targets RFE/RL 02 May 2026 -- The Israeli military said on May 2 it had struck dozens of Hezbollah targets in southern Lebanon amid a fragile cease-fire.
  • Hezbollah insists on five conditions for Lebanon-Israel ceasefire to hold: Lawmaker Press TV 02 May 2026 -- A senior Lebanese lawmaker from the political wing of the Hezbollah resistance movement says a five-point plan must be implemented for the ceasefire between the Beirut government and Israel to hold, and secure complete cessation of hostilities.

  • Attack on Gaza-bound aid flotilla sign of Israel's moral decadence: Hamas Press TV 02 May 2026 -- The Palestinian Hamas resistance movement has strongly condemned the violent interception of vessels of a Gaza-bound aid flotilla and detention of pro-Palestinian activists, describing the attack as a clear sign of the Tel Aviv regime's moral decadence.

    • Other Conflicts

  • Global demining work strained by rising conflicts and shrinking aid UN News 02 May 2026 -- Demining experts from around the world have been sharing their collective shock at the widespread and growing threat from unexploded ordnance, the new head of the UN Mine Action Service (UNMAS) said this week.

  • Statement by the Spokesperson Pakistan Ministry of Foreign Affairs 02 May 2026 -- In response to media queries regarding a social media post by the British Special Representative for Afghanistan, the Spokesperson, Mr Tahir Andrabi, stated the following:

  • "They are attempting to undermine the fragile peace process and harm interstate relations" — STATEMENT Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 02 May 2026 -- The Milli Majlis Temporary Commission on Foreign Interference and Hybrid Threats has issued a statement regarding the information campaign carried out by pro-Armenian circles against Azerbaijan.

News Reports

  • U.S. lawmakers propose China threat resolution ahead of Trump-Xi summit CNA 02 May 2026 -- U.S. lawmakers across party lines on Friday introduced a resolution to express their concerns over China's threats toward Taiwan, with peace and stability across the Taiwan Strait as its stated focus, ahead of a planned summit between U.S. President Donald Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping in mid-May.
  • China blocks US sanctions on refineries buying Iranian oil Press TV 02 May 2026 -- China has blocked US sanctions imposed on its refineries over purchasing Iranian oil, saying that Washington's measures "shall not be complied with."
  • HKSAR gov't decries attempts to whitewash Jimmy Lai Global Times 02 May 2026 -- The Hong Kong Special Administrative Region (HKSAR) government on Friday strongly condemned attempts made by an anti-China organization and foreign media to sugarcoat the criminal acts of Jimmy Lai Chee-ying by releasing a so-called press freedom index and presenting him with a so-called "award."

  • President Lai arrives in Eswatini CNA 02 May 2026 -- President Lai Ching-te (賴清德) announced Saturday that he had arrived in Eswatini, after an earlier planned visit to Taiwan's sole African ally was suspended because of revoked overflight permits.
  • Canadian MPs defiant over Chinese envoy's 'warning' on Taiwan CNA 02 May 2026 -- A Chinese diplomat's warning against Canadian parliamentarians visiting Taiwan and Canadian naval vessels transiting the Taiwan Strait has sparked a backlash among Canadian lawmakers.
  • Lai 'smuggles' himself out of Taiwan island hours after Yilan earthquake, performing laughable stunt, says FM on Lai's private jet trip to Eswatini Global Times 02 May 2026 -- In response to Taiwan regional leader Lai Ching-te's travel to Eswatini on its King's private jet on Saturday, a Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson said on Saturday that just hours within the Yilan earthquake, Lai ditched the people in Taiwan who were still reeling from the disaster and sneaked onto a foreign plane to "smuggle" himself out of the island, squandering taxpayers' money and performing a laughable stunt in front of the world. That just added yet another episode to the scandalous "Taiwan independence" separatism.
  • Lai's despicable conduct, like rat running across street, bound to be ridiculed by the world: mainland spokesperson on Lai's reported sneaking into Eswatini by plane Global Times 02 May 2026 -- Earlier, as relevant countries adhered to the one-China principle, Lai Ching-te's attempt to visit Eswatini by chartered plane had to be called off. Unwilling to accept defeat, Lai nonetheless stooped to sneaking out of Taiwan island in a disgraceful manner to reach Eswatini, Chen Binhua, spokesperson for the Taiwan Affairs Office of the State Council, made the remarks on Saturday, in response to media reports that said Lai sneaked onto a plane of Eswatini and fled to the country.
  • China slams Japanese politicians' statement regarding China's Taiwan People's Daily 02 May 2026 -- Chinese Foreign Ministry Spokesperson Lin Jian on Thursday slammed certain Japanese politicians for their statements on the Taiwan region following Taiwan's leader Lai Ching-te's failed attempt to visit Eswatini.
  • Foreign Ministry Spokesperson's Remarks on Lai Ching-te's Travel to Eswatini on its King's Private Jet PRC MofA 02 May 2026
  • PLA activities in the waters and airspace around Taiwan ROC MND 02 May 2026

  • Two men convicted of spying for Israel's Mossad executed in northwestern Iran Press TV 02 May 2026 -- Two men convicted of spying for the Israeli regime's Mossad intelligence service have been executed in Iran's northwestern province of West Azarbaijan.
  • Iran Executes Two Men Accused Of Spying For Israel RFE/RL 02 May 2026 -- Two men convicted of spying for Israel were executed early on May 2, the judiciary-run Mizan news agency reported, as rights organizations warn of a sharp escalation in the use of capital punishment.
  • Iranian Nobel Laureate Narges Mohammadi 'On Brink Of Death' RFE/RL 02 May 2026 -- Jailed Iranian Nobel Peace Prize winner Narges Mohammadi has been transferred to intensive care and is "on the brink of death," her brother Hamidreza Mohammadi told RFE/RL's Radio Farda.

  • Foreign Policy Speech by Prime Minister TAKAICHI Sanae Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Japan 02 May 2026 -- On May 2, commencing at 3:15 p.m. (5:15 p.m., Japan time) for approximately 25 minutes, Ms. TAKAICHI Sanae, Prime Minister of Japan, who is visiting Viet Nam, delivered a foreign policy speech at Vietnam National University, Hanoi, including the evolution of the Free and Open Indo-Pacific (FOIP).

  • Seven OPEC+ Countries Plan to Increase Oil Production Limits Sputnik 02 May 2026 -- Russia, Saudi Arabia, Iraq, Kuwait, Algeria, Kazakhstan, and Oman have preliminarily agreed to increase the target production by approximately 188,000 barrels per day in June, despite the UAE leaving the alliance, media reported.
